headline.text: L2 ML2 has been down *all shift* after HMI froze - motor swap suggests the problem isn't the motor itself.

per_plant_summary.L1: S3 fought piece count issues all shift with multiple temper adjustments and an accidental buffer depletion, back running by 20:36. S2 found metal in a jar that X-ray caught but the metal detector missed; maintenance inspected all equipment and cleared the line to restart at 17:44.
per_plant_summary.L2: ML2 down all shift after HMI froze at 15:59 - motor swap with ML3 suggests the issue isn't the motor but something else on the Theegarten. FFS had two brief printer stoppages and a checkweigher fault, all recovered same-shift.
per_plant_summary.L3: ML1 metal detector belt shifted repeatedly due to loose set screws on the pulley shaft - fixed at 18:09, line back running at 22:02. ML4 gondola lost position three times requiring re-zeroing; root cause appears to be bent carriage slats and ramp misalignment. MF Matrix scale vibration resolved after Nathan found three linear feeders set up incorrectly.

watchlist.reason: Batch 20009 still too soft to run on TW since Friday - scheduled to run Monday but no update on product readiness
S3 jar filling conveyor still failing allergen test for Level 4 clean since 2026-05-09 - blocks production

notes.text: Contractors cutting concrete on Line 1 for drain work - area taped off and tent in place per Marco Rodriguez at 16:55
Pretzel supply constraint - scheduled to run until May 18 but only have pretzels through Wednesday; 3 loads (27,000 lb each) arriving this week
Jay Alba has 2 servo motors inbound for ML2 - one from UK, one from Sparks NV (expected tomorrow) - though motor swap suggests issue may not be motor
